title: Wildlife Conservation
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Wildlife conservation has (0) become increasingly important as human activities continue to threaten natural habitats. Many species are now (1) on the verge of extinction due to deforestation, pollution, and climate change. Conservation organisations are working tirelessly to (2) raise awareness about these issues and protect endangered animals. National parks and nature reserves have been (3) set up around the world to provide safe havens for wildlife. Scientists are also (4) carrying out research to better understand animal behaviour and develop effective protection strategies. Breeding programmes have helped to (5) bring back several species from the brink of extinction. However, conservation efforts often (6) come up against challenges such as limited funding and political obstacles. Local communities play a crucial role in wildlife protection, as they can (7) look after the natural areas near their homes. Education is essential to ensure that future generations (8) take responsibility for preserving the planet's biodiversity for years to come.

example.explanation: 'Become' es el verbo correcto para indicar un cambio de estado.

questions.explanation: 'On the verge of' es la expresión fija que significa estar a punto de algo.
'Raise awareness' es la colocación correcta para aumentar la concienciación.
'Set up' significa establecer o crear algo nuevo.
'Carrying out research' es la colocación correcta para realizar investigación.
'Bring back' significa hacer que algo vuelva a existir o prosperar.
'Come up against' significa enfrentarse a problemas u obstáculos.
'Look after' es el phrasal verb más común para cuidar de algo.
'Take responsibility for' es la expresión fija para asumir responsabilidad.
